task.name: Stop Signal Task (RDoC)
reference_literature: Logan, G. D., & Cowan, W. B. (1984). On the ability to inhibit thought and action. Psychological Review, 91, 295-327.
Verbruggen, F., & Logan, G. D. (2008). Response inhibition in the stop-signal paradigm. TiCS, 12, 418-424.
Verbruggen et al. (2019). A consensus guide to capturing the ability to inhibit actions and impulsive behaviors in the stop-signal task. eLife.

stimuli.description: Shape with stop-signal star overlay; participant must withhold response

response.response_key_js: (function(){var img=document.querySelector('#jspsych-stop-signal-stimulus img'); if(!img) return ','; var src=img.src||''; var gi=(window.efVars&&window.efVars.group_index!=null)?window.efVars.group_index:1; if(gi>=0 && gi<=4){ return src.indexOf('circle')!==-1 ? ',' : '.'; } else { return src.indexOf('square')!==-1 ? ',' : '.'; }})()

stimuli.description: Go trial showing a shape (circle or square) without stop signal

response.response_key_js: (function(){var img=document.querySelector('#jspsych-stop-signal-stimulus img'); if(!img) return ','; var src=img.src||''; var gi=(window.efVars&&window.efVars.group_index!=null)?window.efVars.group_index:1; if(gi>=0 && gi<=4){ return src.indexOf('circle')!==-1 ? ',' : '.'; } else { return src.indexOf('square')!==-1 ? ',' : '.'; }})()

autocorrelation.rationale: Modest lag-1 autocorrelation typical of RT series (Gilden, 2001; Wagenmakers et al., 2004)

fatigue_drift.rationale: Slight RT drift over ~180 test trials in a ~10 min task

post_error_slowing.rationale: Post-error slowing is well-documented in stop-signal and choice RT tasks (Rabbitt, 1966; Dutilh et al., 2012)

condition_repetition.rationale: Not a primary manipulation in stop-signal task

pink_noise.rationale: 1/f structure is a robust feature of RT series (Gilden, 2001; Van Orden et al., 2003)

post_interrupt_slowing.rationale: Post-stop-signal slowing is well-documented (Verbruggen & Logan, 2008; Bissett & Logan, 2011)

between_subject_jitter.rationale: Typical between-subject variability in stop-signal RTs (Verbruggen et al., 2019 consensus guide)

pilot.rationale: Practice block contains 6 trials with both go and stop conditions (2:1 ratio)

phase_detection.complete: (function(){var el=document.querySelector('#jspsych-content, body'); if(!el) return false; var t=el.innerText||''; return /Thanks for completing this task/i.test(t) || document.getElementById('finished_message')!==null;})()
phase_detection.feedback: (function(){var el=document.querySelector('#jspsych-content'); if(!el) return false; var t=el.innerText||''; return (/Please take this time to read your feedback/i.test(t) || /You have completed \d+ out of/i.test(t)) && document.querySelector('#jspsych-stop-signal-stimulus')===null;})()

attention_check.response_js: (function(){var el=document.querySelector('#jspsych-attention-check-rdoc-stimulus'); if(!el) return 'q'; var t=(el.innerText||'').toLowerCase(); var m=t.match(/press the ([a-z]) key/i); if(m) return m[1].toLowerCase(); if(/first letter of the english alphabet/i.test(t)) return 'a'; if(/second letter of the english alphabet/i.test(t)) return 'b'; if(/third letter of the english alphabet/i.test(t)) return 'c'; if(/third to last letter/i.test(t)) return 'x'; if(/second to last letter/i.test(t)) return 'y'; if(/last letter of the english alphabet/i.test(t)) return 'z'; return 'q';})()
